name: Amber Grant for Women
description: Monthly $10,000 grants for women-owned small businesses across multiple categories (Amber, Startup, and Business Category), with year-end winners receiving an additional $50,000. A $15 one-time application fee considers applicants for all monthly categories.
notes: Requires a $15 application fee. Applications accepted year-round with monthly deadlines.

name: FedEx Small Business Grant Contest
description: Annual contest awarding 12 U.S. small businesses cash grants (one $50,000 grand prize, one $30,000 second place, ten $15,000 third-place awards) plus FedEx Office print services. Recurs annually; the 2026 cycle closed in March.
notes: Requires an active FedEx business shipping account. Nonprofits and franchises ineligible. Recurs annually.

name: Comcast RISE
description: Provides small businesses a $5,000 monetary grant plus a technology makeover, marketing/advertising support, and business consultation. Recurs annually; applications typically open mid-year.
notes: Business must be 2+ years old with 100 or fewer employees. 2026 deadline/regions not confirmed in sources; status estimated closed as of 2026-06-11. Recurs.

name: SBA Growth Accelerator Fund Competition (GAFC)
description: Two-stage competition awarding entrepreneur support organizations (accelerators, universities, nonprofits) Stage One (~$50,000) and Stage Two (~$150,000) to strengthen R&D-focused small business innovation ecosystems. Recurs annually.
notes: Funds ecosystem-building organizations rather than individual businesses. Award amounts vary by fiscal year; FY2025 figures cited. Recurs annually.

name: Grants for Arts Projects
description: Federal grants supporting arts projects by nonprofit 501(c)(3) organizations, ranging $10,000-$100,000 (subgranting agencies up to $150,000). Requires a 1:1 nonfederal match. Recurs with two deadlines annually.
notes: Applicant must have 5+ years of arts programming. Second FY2026 deadline is July 9, 2026; first deadline (Feb 12, 2026) has passed.

name: Small Business Growth Fund
description: Grants of $5,000-$25,000 for U.S. for-profit small businesses with $5,000-$5,000,000 in annual gross revenue. Hello Alice runs multiple rolling and partner-sponsored grant programs throughout the year.
notes: Hello Alice hosts many concurrent partner grants with varying deadlines; the flagship Growth Fund is the reference here. No application fee.

name: NASE Growth Grant
description: Awards up to $4,000 to NASE member small businesses to fund a specific business need such as marketing, equipment, or hiring. Applications accepted year-round with quarterly review cycles.
notes: Requires NASE membership in good standing. Monthly members must wait 90 days after joining.

name: Value-Added Producer Grant (VAPG)
description: Federal grants helping agricultural producers develop value-added products from raw commodities, with planning grants up to $50,000 and working capital grants up to $200,000. Requires a 1:1 match. Recurs annually.
notes: FY2026 application window (Feb 17-Apr 22, 2026) has closed; program recurs annually.

name: Military Entrepreneur Challenge
description: Pitch competition awarding veteran, military spouse, and Gold Star family entrepreneurs capital grants ($15,000 first, $6,000 second, $4,000 third) plus in-kind legal services. Recurs annually with regional events.
notes: Eligibility limited to veterans, military spouses, and Gold Star families. Held as recurring regional competitions; specific 2026 event dates vary.

name: Patagonia Corporate Environmental Grants
description: Grants of roughly $5,000-$20,000 to small grassroots 501(c)(3) environmental organizations running direct-action campaigns to protect local habitats and frontline communities. Recurs annually (fiscal year May 1-Apr 30).
notes: One proposal per group per fiscal year. FY2026 deadline of Apr 30, 2026 has passed; recurs annually.

name: Awesome Foundation Micro-Grants
description: No-strings-attached $1,000 micro-grants awarded monthly by autonomous local chapters to individuals, groups, and organizations for projects spanning arts, technology, and community development.
notes: Open to anyone (individuals, businesses, nonprofits). Deadlines and themes vary by chapter; generally monthly.

name: Scale-Up Pitch Challenge
description: Annual pitch competition awarding non-dilutive cash prizes up to $50,000 to early-stage Black-founded startups (in operation no more than ten years). Recurs annually, typically culminating at the NBMBAA conference.
notes: Founder must be Black, a U.S. citizen, and 18+. Recurs annually; specific 2026 deadline not confirmed in sources.

name: The Coramino Fund Grants
description: $10,000 grants for small businesses (5 years or younger, up to 30 employees) in Atlanta, Chicago, Detroit, Houston, and Los Angeles, co-founded by Kevin Hart. Recurs in rounds. The 2026 round closed April 6.
notes: Geographically limited to five cities. 2026 round closed; program runs in recurring rounds.

name: Spark Good Local Grants
description: Local cash grants of $250-$5,000 from Walmart stores, Sam's Clubs, and distribution centers to nonprofits serving the local community in areas like animal welfare, elder services, and clean-up. Runs in three application cycles annually.
notes: Requires a verified Spark Good account. 2026 final cycle (Cycle 3) runs Aug 1-Nov 30, 2026; status open reflects an active cycle as of 2026-06-11.

name: Verizon Small Business Digital Ready National Grant
description: $10,000 grants for U.S. for-profit small businesses, awarded to 10 businesses monthly June-December 2026. Applicants must complete two courses or events on the Digital Ready platform to unlock the application.
notes: Application stays under consideration for the full year once submitted. Free to apply; requires completing platform coursework.

name: SBIR/STTR Phase I Awards
description: Federal R&D grants for U.S. small businesses pursuing innovative technology with commercial potential, administered across agencies (NSF, NIH, DOE, USDA, etc.) with varying Phase I ceilings up to roughly $323,090.
notes: Each participating agency sets its own award amounts and solicitation deadlines; the cited ceiling is the SBA guideline as of April 2026.

name: IFundWomen Universal Grant Application
description: A single rolling application that matches women-owned and other small businesses to partner grant opportunities ranging $1,000-$25,000 (some partner awards higher), plus coaching and funding resources.
notes: Free to apply. Award amounts and criteria depend on the matched partner grant; database matching is ongoing.

name: Neighborhood Builders
description: Provides selected 501(c)(3) nonprofits $200,000 in general operating support over two years plus leadership training for the executive director and an emerging leader. Recurs annually in participating markets.
notes: 2026 application window is June 1-July 1, 2026, so it is open as of 2026-06-11. Available in select markets only.
